name: The Hero's Journey (Community)
description: Campbell's classic monomyth framework, adapted for modern stories.
author: Community
arcDescription: The hero leaves the ordinary world, survives trials, and returns transformed.

sections.title: The Ordinary World
sections.description: Establish the hero's everyday life and its shortcomings before the adventure begins.

sections.title: The Call to Adventure
sections.description: A problem or challenge presents itself, disrupting the status quo.

sections.title: Refusal of the Call
sections.description: The hero hesitates, showing the stakes and internal resistance.

sections.title: Meeting the Mentor
sections.description: A wise figure provides guidance, tools, or motivation to face the journey.

sections.title: Crossing the First Threshold
sections.description: The hero commits to the adventure and enters the unknown world.

sections.title: Tests, Allies and Enemies
sections.description: The hero faces a series of challenges, forms alliances, and identifies antagonists.

sections.title: The Innermost Cave
sections.description: The hero approaches the most dangerous place — physical or psychological.

sections.title: The Supreme Ordeal
sections.description: The central crisis. The hero faces death or their greatest fear.

sections.title: Reward
sections.description: Having survived, the hero seizes the prize — a treasure, knowledge, or reconciliation.

sections.title: The Road Back
sections.description: The hero begins the return, but the antagonistic force may not be finished.

sections.title: Resurrection
sections.description: The climactic final test. The hero is transformed by the experience.

sections.title: Return with the Elixir
sections.description: The hero returns home changed, carrying something of value to share with the world.

name: Dark Romance
description: Gothic love story with suspense and dark secrets. Contains mature themes — use only if it fits your story and audience.
author: NightWriter42
arcDescription: Forbidden love in a world full of secrets — suspense and passion in balance.

sections.title: First Encounter in the Dark
sections.description: The two protagonists meet under charged, ambiguous circumstances that feel dangerous and magnetic.

sections.title: The Hidden Secret
sections.description: Something about the love interest is concealed — a past, a nature, a bargain.

sections.title: Attraction Despite Warning
sections.description: The protagonist is drawn closer despite every rational signal to stay away.

sections.title: The First Promise
sections.description: A bond forms — reluctant, conditional, or unspoken — that neither can easily break.

sections.title: Discovery of True Nature
sections.description: The secret is revealed, reshaping everything the protagonist believed.

sections.title: Betrayal and Separation
sections.description: The revelation shatters trust. Distance — emotional or physical — follows.

sections.title: The Decision
sections.description: The protagonist must choose: walk away forever, or accept darkness alongside the love.

sections.title: Sacrifice and Redemption
sections.description: Love demands a price. The resolution is earned, not given — and costs something real.

name: Countdown Thriller
description: High tension with a ticking clock — time pressure from first page to last.
author: ThrillMaster
arcDescription: A protagonist with limited time, an antagonist operating in the shadows.

sections.title: The Bomb Ticks (Setup)
sections.description: Establish the protagonist, the stakes, and the ticking clock in the opening pages.

sections.title: First Lead
sections.description: The protagonist uncovers the first real clue — incomplete, misleading, but enough to act on.

sections.title: False Trail
sections.description: The lead points in the wrong direction. Time is lost. The antagonist gains ground.

sections.title: Escalation — First Casualty
sections.description: The threat becomes real. Someone pays a price, and the stakes are no longer abstract.

sections.title: Turning Point: Revelation
sections.description: A discovery reframes everything. The protagonist now knows what they are actually facing.

sections.title: Countdown Accelerates
sections.description: The pace intensifies. Every chapter the clock ticks louder. Options narrow.

sections.title: All Lost — Heroine Alone
sections.description: Allies fall, resources are gone, and the protagonist faces the final stretch alone.

sections.title: Final Confrontation
sections.description: The climax: protagonist against antagonist with everything on the line and seconds to spare.

sections.title: Resolution & Aftermath
sections.description: The dust settles. Show what was saved, what was lost, and where the protagonist lands.

name: Cozy Mystery
description: A gentle puzzle in a charming small town — light tone, wit over gore.
author: TeaAndClues
arcDescription: An amateur detective solves village mysteries between baking and gossip.

sections.title: The Charming Small Town
sections.description: Introduce the warm, quirky setting and its lovable cast of regulars. Establish the amateur sleuth's daily life.

sections.title: The Strange Event
sections.description: Something disrupts the peace — a death, a theft, a disappearance. The police are baffled or indifferent.

sections.title: Suspicious Villagers
sections.description: Every neighbor has a reason, a secret, or an alibi that doesn't quite hold up.

sections.title: First Investigations
sections.description: The protagonist starts asking questions — over tea, during book club, at the bakery counter.

sections.title: Red Herring
sections.description: A perfectly suspicious suspect is cleared, throwing the investigation off balance.

sections.title: Personal Danger
sections.description: The sleuth gets too close. A warning arrives — subtle but unmistakable.

sections.title: The Crucial Clue
sections.description: A detail overlooked earlier suddenly makes sense. Everything clicks into place.

sections.title: Resolution at the Village Festival
sections.description: The culprit is revealed in a satisfying, public setting. The town breathes again.

name: First Contact Sci-Fi
description: Humanity meets intelligent life for the first time — ideas-first science fiction.
author: StargazerX
arcDescription: What does it mean to be human — when you must explain it to someone utterly unlike you?

sections.title: The Signal
sections.description: An anomaly is detected — a pattern too ordered to be natural. The world doesn't know yet.

sections.title: Global Response
sections.description: Governments, scientists, and factions react with wonder, fear, and competing agendas.

sections.title: The First Message
sections.description: Contact is made. The message is real, but its meaning is uncertain and contested.

sections.title: Diplomatic Crisis
sections.description: Factions within humanity disagree on how to respond — and who speaks for Earth.

sections.title: Misunderstanding and Escalation
sections.description: A cultural or conceptual gap causes a dangerous misread on one or both sides.

sections.title: The Breakthrough
sections.description: A human (or alien) builds a bridge through art, mathematics, or shared vulnerability.

sections.title: True Intentions
sections.description: The aliens' real purpose is revealed — more complex, stranger, or sadder than assumed.

sections.title: Decision for Humanity
sections.description: Humanity must choose: open the door, close it, or do something unprecedented.

sections.title: Aftermath — A Changed World
sections.description: The world is different now. Show what the contact cost, what it gave, and what comes next.

name: Coming-of-Age Arc
description: A literary bildungsroman — innocence, failure, love, and mature selfhood.
author: LitProfessor
arcDescription: From youthful naivety to adult identity — through failure, love, and loss.

sections.title: Childhood and Innocence
sections.description: Show the protagonist's world as they understand it — small, safe, incomplete.

sections.title: First Confrontation with the World
sections.description: Something outside the protected sphere intrudes. The protagonist's understanding cracks.

sections.title: Mentor and Guide
sections.description: A figure offers wisdom, but imperfectly — the protagonist must ultimately make their own choices.

sections.title: First Love / First Defeat
sections.description: An intense experience of attachment and loss. The emotional core of the bildungsroman.

sections.title: Identity Crisis
sections.description: Who am I? The protagonist cannot answer. External certainties are gone.

sections.title: Rebellion and Consequences
sections.description: The protagonist acts against the established order — and faces the real cost of freedom.

sections.title: Rock Bottom
sections.description: The lowest point. Everything the protagonist tried has failed or proven hollow.

sections.title: Reconciliation with the Past
sections.description: The protagonist makes peace — with a parent, a mistake, a lost version of themselves.

sections.title: Coming-of-Age Trial
sections.description: One final test that demonstrates how far the protagonist has genuinely grown.

sections.title: Setting Out into the Future
sections.description: The story ends not with arrival but with departure — into an uncertain but chosen life.

name: Shakespearean Five-Act Structure
description: The classic dramatic scaffold used by Shakespeare — ideal for stage-like intensity and formal tragic or comedic arcs.
author: TheatreMajor
arcDescription: Exposition, rising action, climax, falling action, catastrophe — the eternal rhythm of drama.

sections.title: Act I — Exposition
sections.description: Introduce the world, the central characters, and the inciting conflict. Plant the seeds of both tragedy and resolution.

sections.title: Act II — Rising Action
sections.description: Complications multiply. The protagonist's situation grows more entangled. Alliances form and fray.

sections.title: Act III — Climax
sections.description: The turning point — the highest point of tension, the moment of decision, the pivot on which everything turns.

sections.title: Act IV — Falling Action
sections.description: Consequences cascade. Events move toward the inevitable conclusion. Reversals and revelations accelerate.

sections.title: Act V — Catastrophe or Resolution
sections.description: The final movement: tragedy claims its toll, or comedy finds its resolution. Order is restored — or forever shattered.

name: Psychological Horror
description: Horror that lives inside the mind — unreliable narrators, creeping dread, and questions about what is real.
author: NightmareInk
arcDescription: The monster is real — but so is the possibility that the protagonist created it.

sections.title: Everything Seems Normal
sections.description: Open on a life that feels ordinary — but the prose should make the reader uneasy without knowing why.

sections.title: The First Wrong Note
sections.description: One small detail that doesn't add up. The protagonist explains it away. The reader cannot.

sections.title: Escalation of Strangeness
sections.description: Events become harder to rationalize. The protagonist's grip on normality visibly slips.

sections.title: Reality Questioned
sections.description: Is the threat external or internal? Plant genuine doubt — the reader should not be sure.

sections.title: Isolation
sections.description: The protagonist becomes cut off — from allies, from reason, from their previous self.

sections.title: The Abyss Looks Back
sections.description: The horror fully reveals itself. Whether real or imagined, it is now undeniable and intimate.

sections.title: No Safe Escape
sections.description: Every option the protagonist had is gone. The climax forces a confrontation they cannot avoid.

sections.title: Aftermath — Broken or Changed
sections.description: The resolution does not return to normal. Something fundamental has been altered, lost, or uncovered.

name: Romantic Comedy
description: Two people who should not fall in love do exactly that — after enough obstacles, misunderstandings, and comic setbacks.
author: HappyEndings
arcDescription: Love finds a way — despite bad timing, wrong assumptions, and the best efforts of both parties.

sections.title: Meet (Badly)
sections.description: The two leads meet in circumstances that create immediate friction, misunderstanding, or competition.

sections.title: Forced Together
sections.description: Circumstance, a job, a bet, or a shared goal throws them into proximity they cannot escape.

sections.title: Walls Go Up
sections.description: Both characters resist the growing attraction — they each have a reason it cannot work.

sections.title: Walls Come Down (Briefly)
sections.description: A genuine moment of connection. They glimpse who the other actually is beneath the surface.

sections.title: The Misunderstanding
sections.description: A classic romcom beat: a lie, a secret, a mistaken assumption — one that tears them apart right when things were good.

sections.title: All Is Lost (In Love)
sections.description: They are apart. The protagonist realizes they are miserable without this person they tried not to want.

sections.title: The Grand Gesture
sections.description: One of them does something brave, ridiculous, or heartfelt that cannot be misinterpreted.

sections.title: The Happy Ending
sections.description: They come together — honestly, without pretense — and the reader believes it will last.

name: Seven-Point Story Structure
description: Dan Wells' plotting method — work backwards from the resolution, build forward from the hook.
author: PlotArchitect
arcDescription: Seven precise moments that guarantee a story moves forward with purpose and payoff.

sections.title: Hook — Starting State
sections.description: Show the protagonist's world as it is before the story changes anything. This is the opposite of where they will end.

sections.title: Plot Turn 1 — Call to Action
sections.description: Something pushes the protagonist out of their starting state and into the story proper.

sections.title: Pinch Point 1 — Pressure Applied
sections.description: The antagonist or the conflict applies real force. The protagonist feels the weight of what they are facing.

sections.title: Midpoint — Reaction to Action
sections.description: The protagonist stops reacting and starts acting. A revelation or decision shifts them from passive to active.

sections.title: Pinch Point 2 — Greatest Pressure
sections.description: The antagonist exerts maximum force. Things look worst here — for the protagonist and the reader.

sections.title: Plot Turn 2 — Final Clue
sections.description: The protagonist gains the final piece they need — knowledge, courage, or a tool — to win.

sections.title: Resolution — End State
sections.description: The protagonist reaches the state that is the opposite of where they started. The conflict is resolved.

name: Historical Epic
description: Sweeping fiction rooted in a real era — intimate personal story braided with the forces of history.
author: Archivist
arcDescription: One life caught in the tide of history — changed by events too large to control, too important to ignore.

sections.title: A World on the Brink
sections.description: Establish the historical era with visceral, specific detail. Show the characters before the upheaval.

sections.title: The Turning Event
sections.description: History moves. The protagonist is no longer outside events — they are inside them.

sections.title: Caught Between Powers
sections.description: The character navigates forces — political, military, social — larger than any individual.

sections.title: The Cost of Survival
sections.description: Staying alive or free requires compromise. Show what the character gives up and what they protect.

sections.title: Lost World Mourned
sections.description: What existed before is gone — a place, a community, a way of life. Let the character grieve it honestly.

sections.title: Alliance and Betrayal
sections.description: In chaos, alliances form across unexpected lines. Some hold; some break at the worst moment.

sections.title: The Climactic Moment of History
sections.description: The protagonist stands at the intersection of personal choice and historical event.

sections.title: What Survives
sections.description: Not triumph — what remains. Show what was carried through the storm and what it cost to carry it.

name: Nonlinear / Experimental Narrative
description: A fractured timeline, multiple POVs, or genre-bending form — for writers who want structure that feels alive.
author: AvantGarde
arcDescription: The reader assembles the story from fragments — each piece reveals something the others conceal.

sections.title: Fragment A — Present Tense (Aftermath)
sections.description: Open in the aftermath. The reader does not yet know what happened — only that something did.

sections.title: Fragment B — Past (Origin)
sections.description: Jump back. Show who these characters were before. Establish what they had to lose.

sections.title: Fragment C — Parallel Thread
sections.description: A second voice, time, or perspective begins. Its relationship to the first is unclear — and that tension is the engine.

sections.title: Convergence Point 1
sections.description: The threads make contact for the first time. A shared detail, event, or character connects them.

sections.title: Revelation Layer 1
sections.description: Something the reader thought they understood is reframed. The fragment structure is not decoration — it hides meaning.

sections.title: Convergence Point 2
sections.description: The timelines or voices approach each other. The gap between them narrows with growing tension.

sections.title: Revelation Layer 2 — The Core Truth
sections.description: What the structure was protecting is exposed. This is the emotional center the fragments were circling.

sections.title: Resolution — All Threads Land
sections.description: The narrative does not need to be linear to be complete. Find the shape that makes all the pieces feel inevitable.

name: Western
description: Dust, honor, and moral ambiguity — the American frontier as moral landscape.
author: SaddleScribe
arcDescription: A lone figure against a lawless land — but the real conflict is the code they carry inside.

sections.title: Riding Into Town
sections.description: Establish the setting and the protagonist's arrival or reawakening. Something is wrong here — and they are the wrong person to ignore it.

sections.title: The Problem No One Else Will Face
sections.description: The community has a threat. Everyone knows. No one is willing to stand against it alone.

sections.title: The Protagonist's Code
sections.description: Show the protagonist's internal law: what they will and will not do, and why. This is the story's moral engine.

sections.title: The Antagonist's Power
sections.description: The villain is established — real, capable, and in some ways understandable. They are not simply evil.

sections.title: Alliances Formed Under Pressure
sections.description: An unlikely partner, a local who believes, a surprising loyalty — and one betrayal.

sections.title: The Confrontation That Isn't
sections.description: The first major showdown that doesn't resolve anything — someone is not ready, not willing, or not there yet.

sections.title: The Final Reckoning
sections.description: Dust street, high noon, or something more modern — the climax is earned by everything that came before.

sections.title: Riding Out
sections.description: The protagonist doesn't get to stay. The world is better — or just different. They move on.

name: Memoir Arc
description: A real life told with a novelist's eye — structured for emotional truth, not chronological completeness.
author: TrueVoice
arcDescription: The narrator you are now looking back at the person you were — and finding the meaning in between.

sections.title: The Narrator's Present Voice
sections.description: Establish who you are now — with the perspective that makes this story worth telling.

sections.title: The World Before
sections.description: Set the scene of the past. Give the reader the context and stakes before the central experience.

sections.title: The Inciting Experience
sections.description: The event, relationship, or realization that made this life into a story that needed to be told.

sections.title: Deepening — What It Cost
sections.description: Show the experience from the inside: the confusion, the feeling, the human texture of living through it.

sections.title: The Person I Became (And Resisted)
sections.description: Change is rarely welcome. Show the resistance — the habits, the denials, the person you fought to stay.

sections.title: The Turning Realization
sections.description: The moment of genuine insight — not resolution yet, but understanding. The fog lifts on something.

sections.title: Reckoning with Others
sections.description: The people in a memoir are real. How you portray them, forgive them, or hold them accountable is part of the story.

sections.title: The Meaning Made
sections.description: Not a lesson — meaning. What this experience means to you now, without tying it into a bow it does not deserve.
